target.title: fix(telegram): treat explicit empty groups as unset for root fallback in single-account bots
target.body:
## Problem

A Telegram bot configured with a single account and explicit empty `accounts.<X>.groups: {}` silently replaces the root `channels.telegram.groups` allowlist with an empty object, dropping all group memberships. This means any group update is rejected with no match, even though the operator intended to use the root-level groups config.

## Root Cause

In `extensions/telegram/src/account-config.ts`, `account.groups ?? (...)` treats `{}` as truthy. An explicit `groups: {}` on the account is used as-is, overriding root `channelGroups` instead of falling back to it.

## Fix

Replace the `??` guard with an explicit `accountGroupsDefined` check: only use `account.groups` when it has at least one key. Otherwise fall back to root `channels.telegram.groups`.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Single-account Telegram bots with explicit empty `accounts.<X>.groups: {}` dropped the root groups allowlist, rejecting all group updates silently
- Real environment tested: Linux 6.11.0-1016-nvidia x64, Node v22.14.0, openclaw source at extensions/telegram/src/account-config.ts
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node --import tsx /tmp/proof_79427.mjs`
- Evidence after fix:
```
$ node --import tsx /tmp/proof_79427.mjs
merged.groups: {"-1003221749234":{"requireMention":false}}
PASS: empty account.groups falls back to root channelGroups
```
- Observed result after fix: When account has explicit `groups: {}`, merged config uses root `channelGroups` (1 group entry). Before fix, merged config was `{}` (empty, blocking all groups).
- What was not tested: live Telegram group webhook delivery with actual group IDs; hot-reload behavior of account config

Fixes #79427
